title: Bounded gaps between primes in number fields and function fields

sec:1:45.summary: Generalized prime $k$-tuples: every admissible $k$-tuple of integers contains infinitely many translates with all entries prime. Motivating conjecture, restated for the $K / \mathbb{F}_q[t]$ setting.

sec:1:51.summary: Maynard-Tao (2013): for any $m$, there exists $k_0(m)$ such that any admissible $k$-tuple with $k \ge k_0$ has infinitely many translates with $\ge m$ primes. The $\mathbb{Q}$-version restated verbatim.

thm:1.1.summary: Number-field version: for any $m$ and any number field $K$, there is $k_0 = k_0(m, K)$ such that any admissible $k$-tuple of elements in $\mathcal{O}_K$ ($k \ge k_0$) has infinitely many $\alpha \in \mathcal{O}_K$ with $\ge m$ of $\alpha + h_i$ prime in $\mathcal{O}_K$. Direct generalization of Maynard-Tao to rings of integers.

cor:1.2.summary: For totally real $K$: infinitely many prime pairs $\alpha_1, \alpha_2 \in \mathcal{O}_K$ with $|\sigma(\alpha_1 - \alpha_2)| \le 600$ for every embedding $\sigma$. Bounded-gap analog over totally real fields.

thm:1.3.summary: Function-field version $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$: for any $m$, there is $k_0 = k_0(m)$ (independent of $q$!) such that admissible $k$-tuples of polynomials with $k \ge k_0$ have infinitely many $f \in \mathbb{F}_q[t]$ with $\ge m$ of $f + h_i$ irreducible. The function-field analog with $q$-uniform constants.

thm:1.4.summary: Quantitative gap in $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$: explicit fraction $(1/(k_0-1) - 1/(q-1))$ of degree-$d$ elements occur as gaps between consecutive monic irreducibles of degree $n$. Stronger than the $K$-case because of finer $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$ structure.

prop:2.1.summary: Maynard-style sieve weight asymptotic in $\mathcal{O}_K$: with $R = |\mathcal{A}(N)|^{\theta/2-\delta}$ and weights $\lambda$ defined from $F$ supported on simplex $\mathcal{R}_k$ via ideal-multiplied $\mu$-sums, $S_1$ and $S_2$ evaluate analogously to Maynard prop:4.1. The number-field translation.

lem:2.2.summary: Diagonalization $S_1$ in $y$-variables for ideals: $y_{\mathfrak{r}_1,\ldots,\mathfrak{r}_k} = \big(\prod_i \mu(\mathfrak{r}_i)\,\varphi(\mathfrak{r}_i)\big) \sum_{\mathfrak{d}} \lambda_{\mathfrak{d}}/\prod_i |\mathfrak{d}_i|$. Direct ideal-version of Maynard 1311.4600 lem:5.1.

lem:2.3.summary: $S_2$ diagonalization with multiplicative $g(\mathfrak{p}) = |\mathfrak{p}| - 2$ over prime ideals. Ideal-version of Maynard 1311.4600 lem:5.2.

lem:2.4.summary: $y^{(m)}$-to-$y$ bridging when $\mathfrak{r}_m = 1$. Ideal-version of Maynard 1311.4600 lem:5.3.

lem:2.5.summary: Selberg-type weighted ideal sum identity $\sum \mu(\mathfrak{d})^2 g(\mathfrak{d}) G(\log|\mathfrak{d}|/\log z) = \mathfrak{S}\,\log z \int G + O(\cdot)$. Ideal-version of Maynard 1311.4600 lem:6.1.

cor:2.6.summary: Smooth-$F$ sieve sum evaluation in $\mathcal{O}_K$ assembling lem:2.2-2.5 into the analog of Maynard's prop:4.1 conclusion.

thm:2.7.summary: Number-field $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m]$-style reduction: $M_k > 2m/\theta \Rightarrow \mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$ in $\mathcal{O}_K$. Direct lift of Maynard's prop:4.2.

thm:2.8.summary: Function-field $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m]$-style reduction in $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$. Same $M_k > 2m/\theta$ statement, function-field flavor.

lem:3.1.summary: Bombieri-Vinogradov-type level-of-distribution $\theta = 1/2$ in $\mathcal{O}_K$ and $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$. The arithmetic input that lets thm:2.7 / thm:2.8 fire.

edges.note: Totally real specialization with $k = 105$ / $m = 2$ and admissible-tuple diameter 600.
Quantitative gap follows from DHL plus $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$ counting.
Number-field unconditional partial result toward the prime $k$-tuples conjecture in $\mathcal{O}_K$.
Maynard-Tao's $\mathbb{Q}$-version extended to arbitrary number fields.

main_results.description: thm:1.1 (number-field bounded gaps), cor:1.2 (totally-real $H_1 \le 600$ in embedding norm), thm:1.3 (function-field bounded gaps), thm:1.4 (quantitative $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$ gap distribution).

DHL_reductions.description: thm:2.7 ($\mathrm{DHL}[k,m]$ in $\mathcal{O}_K$) + thm:2.8 ($\mathrm{DHL}[k,m]$ in $\mathbb{F}_q[t]$). Direct lifts of Maynard's prop:4.2.

ideal_sieve_machinery.description: prop:2.1 (sieve weight asymptotic for ideals) + cor:2.6 (smooth-$F$ evaluation) + lem:2.2-2.5 (diagonalization and Selberg ideal sums). Verbatim translation of Maynard 1311.4600 prop:4.1 / lem:5.1-5.3 / lem:6.1 to ideals.

Maynard:smallgaps.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:smallgaps.role: method_source — this paper transliterates 1311.4600 to ideals

Tao:blog.title: Tao's parallel discovery of the multidimensional sieve (blog)
Tao:blog.role: context — independent invention of the same sieve

BombieriVinogradov:1965.role: foundational — number-field generalization used in lem:3.1
